task_description:
# Sync a Fork with Multiple Remotes as an Open-Source Maintainer

You are an open-source maintainer who maintains a fork of a project hosted on both GitHub (your fork) and tracks an upstream Gerrit mirror. Your local Jujutsu repository is already set up with two remotes:

- **`origin`** — your GitHub fork (a local bare repo at `~/remotes/origin.git`)
- **`upstream`** — the upstream Gerrit mirror (a local bare repo at `~/remotes/upstream.git`)

Your working repository is at `~/myproject`.

The upstream repository has received new commits on its `main` branch that you need to integrate into your fork. Additionally, you have local work-in-progress commits that need to be rebased on top of the latest upstream changes before pushing to your GitHub fork.

## Starting Environment

When you begin, the repository state is as follows:
- The `upstream` remote has 3 commits on `main`: an initial commit, a "core library" commit, and a new "upstream feature" commit that you have not yet fetched.
- The `origin` remote has 2 commits on `main` (the initial + core library), which is where your fork currently stands.
- Your local repo has a `wip-feature` bookmark pointing to a commit that was branched off the second commit (core library). This commit contains your local work-in-progress changes.
- Your working copy is on an empty change on top of `wip-feature`.

## Your Tasks

### 1. Fetch the latest changes from upstream

Fetch all branches from the `upstream` remote so your local repository knows about the new commit that landed on `upstream/main`.

### 2. Identify the new upstream commit

Use `jj log` to inspect the repository history. Confirm that `upstream@upstream` (the upstream remote bookmark) is ahead of your local `main` bookmark and your `origin@origin` remote bookmark.

### 3. Rebase your WIP feature onto the upstream main

Rebase your `wip-feature` bookmark so that it is based on top of the latest `upstream@upstream` commit (i.e., the tip of `upstream/main`). After rebasing, your `wip-feature` commit should have the upstream feature commit as its parent.

**Hint:** Use `jj rebase -b <bookmark> -d <destination>` to rebase the entire bookmark branch onto the destination.

### 4. Update your local `main` bookmark to match upstream

Move your local `main` bookmark forward so it points to the same commit as `upstream@upstream`. This keeps your local tracking bookmark in sync.

**Hint:** Use `jj bookmark set main -r <revset>` to update the bookmark.

### 5. Push your synced `main` to origin

Push the updated `main` bookmark to your `origin` remote (your GitHub fork), so your fork's `main` is now in sync with upstream.

**Hint:** Use `jj git push --remote origin --bookmark main`.

### 6. Push your rebased WIP feature to origin

Push the `wip-feature` bookmark to your `origin` remote so your GitHub fork has your rebased work-in-progress branch available (e.g., for a draft pull request).

**Hint:** Use `jj git push --remote origin --bookmark wip-feature`.

## Verification

After completing all steps:

- `jj log` should show `wip-feature` bookmark's commit has the upstream feature commit as its parent
- The `main` bookmark should point to the same commit as `upstream@upstream`
- Both `main` and `wip-feature` should be present on `origin` (i.e., `origin@origin` remote bookmarks should reflect the pushed state)
- Running `git -C ~/remotes/origin.git log --oneline main` should show 3 commits (initial, core library, upstream feature)
- Running `git -C ~/remotes/origin.git log --oneline wip-feature` should show 4 commits (initial, core library, upstream feature, wip)

## Notes

- Do not create any new commits with new content — only rebase and push existing work
- The working copy change (the empty change on top of `wip-feature`) does not need to be pushed
- All remote operations use local bare git repos with `file://` paths — no real network access is needed
- Use `jj git fetch --remote <name>` to fetch from a specific remote

truth.local_main_bookmark_points_to_upstream_m: The local 'main' bookmark must point to the same commit as upstream@upstream (the upstream feature commit, with description 'Add upstream feature').
truth.wip_feature_rebased_on_upstream: The 'wip-feature' bookmark must have the upstream feature commit (description: 'Add upstream feature') as its parent commit.
truth.origin_main_has_3_commits: The origin remote's main branch must contain 3 commits: initial commit, core library commit, and upstream feature commit. Verified via: git -C ~/remotes/origin.git log --oneline main | wc -l == 3
truth.origin_wip_feature_has_4_commits: The origin remote's wip-feature branch must contain 4 commits: initial, core library, upstream feature, and wip commit. Verified via: git -C ~/remotes/origin.git log --oneline wip-feature | wc -l == 4
truth.wip_commit_description: The wip-feature bookmark points to a commit with description 'Add WIP feature work'.
truth.upstream_feature_commit_description: The upstream feature commit has description 'Add upstream feature'.
